commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.14: the kindling — a warm ember travels the sentence, lighting each word as you read

Elevate the typewriter node in place (bmf liked the idea: 'needs room to
breathe and some extra sparkle'). Kept the words-brighten identity; raised
the craft, did not recast.

- Room to breathe: beat brisk->dwell (the dim sentence holds for a
  contemplative beat before reading begins) and tempo passage->movement
  (the longest scroll length, so each word is savored one at a time) — a
  ritardando right before the takeover finale.
- Extra sparkle: the hard binary lit/dim flip becomes a continuous dim->lit
  fade plus a warm ember bloom that travels word-to-word as the spark
  kindles each word, trailing a soft afterglow (a bell curve t(1-t) on each
  word's local ignition, so the lit edge reads as ONE travelling light).
  Read words cool to clean white; only the igniting word carries the glow.

Ember rgb is read from the node's accent token via a guarded hexRGB (loud on
a malformed token), not duplicated [LAW:one-source-of-truth][LAW:no-silent-failure].
All scroll-driven from the one scalar n.w; no new WAAPI, reduced-motion
unaffected. Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's full range: zero
page console errors, exactly one glowing word at each scroll position.

mit-styling-aj1.7.13: the inked line — a drafting pen lays its stroke to your scroll (#36)

The draw node kept its approved idea (a line drawn to exactly where you've
scrolled) but its execution was poor: preserveAspectRatio=none stretched the
viewBox non-uniformly, distorting stroke weight and rounding the caps into
ellipses, with a bare white dot riding the tip.

Recast as a drafting pen: meet (uniform fit) restores even ink weight and round
caps; a faint dashed blueprint guide shows where the line is headed; a nib
rotates to the path tangent so it points the way it travels; a glowing wet bead
rides the leading point. Guide and ink share one D string, so the plan and the
inked line cannot diverge. All scroll-driven from the one scalar; no new WAAPI,
so reduced-motion is unaffected. Stays distinct from annotate's settle, count's
figures, waffle's grid and chart's bars.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's full scroll range: ink fills
127->0, nib follows the tangent, bead tracks the tip; zero console errors on
fresh load + full-page sweep.
